title: Projectiles, Edge Triggers, Cooldowns, Lifetimes, and Reuse · Runtime Lens: Fire Cooldown
summary: Held fire spawns unbounded bullets, cooldown uses frame count, expired bullets remain in collision groups, pool reuse keeps old velocity, and rapid restart leaks projectiles. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fire cooldown increment in bounded projectile lifecycle. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Gate shots using simulation time, inclusive boundary tests, pause semantics, and a visible or audible readiness cue.
Convert action edges and held auto-fire policy into deterministic fire requests separate from object creation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-fire-cooldown — Gate shots using simulation time, inclusive boundary tests, pause semantics, and a visible or audible readiness cue.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-projectiles-cooldowns-pools-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-projectiles-cooldowns-pools-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_projectiles_cooldowns_pools_2(requested_shots: int = 12, accepted_shots: int = 5, cooldown_ms: int = 180, lifetime_ms: int = 900, live_cap: int = 6, leaked_projectiles: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if requested_shots < 0 or accepted_shots < 0 or cooldown_ms < 1:
        return False, accepted_shots, "projectile-domain"
    if accepted_shots > requested_shots or accepted_shots > live_cap:
        return False, accepted_shots, "fire-budget"
    if lifetime_ms <= cooldown_ms:
        return False, lifetime_ms, "lifetime-policy"
    if leaked_projectiles > 0:
        return False, leaked_projectiles, "ownership-leak"
    assert accepted_shots <= live_cap
    return True, accepted_shots, "bounded-projectiles"

options.text: For repair the fire cooldown defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Fire Cooldown for bounded projectile lifecycle.

Keep the Fire Cooldown artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the fire cooldown defect evidence boundary for Fire Cooldown.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fire Cooldown model · Theory Fire Cooldown
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Fire Cooldown: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9327 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate edge-triggered firing on cooldown, lifetime, live-count, reuse, and deterministic projectile ownership; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate shots using simulation time, inclusive boundary tests, pause semantics, and a visible or audible readiness cue.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fire Cooldown model · Theory Fire Cooldown.
steps.why: Retrieving Fire Cooldown through the concrete bounded projectile lifecycle case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate shots using simulation time, inclusive boundary tests, pause semantics, and a visible or audible readiness cue.
